Question: Which guest spent the most on reservations at 5-star hotels? Show the full name and total amount spent.

SELECT g.first_name || ' ' || g.last_name AS full_name, SUM(r.total_price) AS total_spent FROM guests g JOIN reservations r ON g.guest_id = r.guest_id JOIN rooms rm ON r.room_id = rm.room_id JOIN hotels h ON rm.hotel_id = h.hotel_id WHERE h.star_rating = 5 GROUP BY g.guest_id ORDER BY total_spent DESC LIMIT 1;
You are a text-to-SQL expert.

Question: Which players won more than 2 matches in grass surface tournaments?
SELECT p.name FROM players p JOIN matches m ON p.player_id = m.winner_id JOIN tournaments t ON m.tournament_id = t.tournament_id WHERE t.surface = 'grass' GROUP BY p.player_id HAVING COUNT(*) > 2;

Question: Which newspapers, founded after 2000, have authors whose specialty is 'Politics' and who have written more than 5 articles in the 'Politics' category? List the newspaper names ordered alphabetically.

SELECT DISTINCT n.name FROM newspapers n JOIN articles a ON n.newspaper_id = a.newspaper_id JOIN authors au ON a.author_id = au.author_id JOIN categories c ON a.category_id = c.category_id WHERE n.founded_year > 2000 AND au.specialty = 'Politics' AND c.name = 'Politics' GROUP BY n.newspaper_id HAVING COUNT(a.article_id) > 5 ORDER BY n.name ASC;
You are a text-to-SQL expert.

Question: Which branch has the highest average price among available copies of books from USA publishers?

SELECT br.name FROM branches br JOIN inventory i ON br.branch_id = i.branch_id JOIN books b ON i.book_id = b.book_id JOIN publishers p ON b.publisher_id = p.publisher_id WHERE i.status = 'available' AND p.country = 'USA' GROUP BY br.branch_id, br.name HAVING AVG(i.price) = (SELECT MAX(avgp) FROM (SELECT AVG(i2.price) AS avgp FROM inventory i2 JOIN books b2 ON i2.book_id = b2.book_id JOIN publishers p2 ON b2.publisher_id = p2.publisher_id JOIN branches br2 ON i2.branch_id = br2.branch_id WHERE i2.status = 'available' AND p2.country = 'USA' GROUP BY i2.branch_id));
You are a text-to-SQL expert.

Question: Which routes operated by airlines from 'USA' have no flight schedules on 2023-10-01? Show route details with airline IATA and airport IATA codes.

SELECT r.route_id, al.iata_code, dep.iata_code AS dep_code, arr.iata_code AS arr_code FROM routes r JOIN airlines al ON r.airline_id = al.airline_id JOIN airports dep ON r.dep_airport_id = dep.airport_id JOIN airports arr ON r.arr_airport_id = arr.airport_id LEFT JOIN flight_schedules fs ON r.route_id = fs.route_id AND fs.departure_date = '2023-10-01' WHERE al.country = 'USA' AND fs.schedule_id IS NULL;
You are a text-to-SQL expert.

Question: Which customers booked more tickets than the average number of tickets per booking for action movie screenings?

Question: Which providers located in 'New York' have total claimed amounts over 10000 from policies expiring after 2024-01-01?

Question: List the top 3 airlines by number of delayed flight schedules in 2023.

SELECT a.name, COUNT(*) AS num_delayed FROM flight_schedules s JOIN flights f ON s.flight_id = f.flight_id JOIN airlines a ON f.airline_id = a.airline_id WHERE s.status = 'delayed' AND strftime('%Y', s.departure_date) = '2023' GROUP BY a.airline_id, a.name ORDER BY num_delayed DESC LIMIT 3;
You are a text-to-SQL expert.

Question: Which player scored the most goals in the Premier League? Show the player name and goal count.

SELECT p.name, COUNT(g.goal_id) AS goals FROM players p JOIN goals g ON p.player_id = g.player_id JOIN matches m ON g.match_id = m.match_id JOIN leagues l ON m.league_id = l.league_id WHERE l.name = 'Premier League' GROUP BY p.player_id ORDER BY goals DESC LIMIT 1;

Question: Which players have won more matches than the average number of wins among players who have won at least one match?

Question: What is the average number of students per route for routes longer than 10 km serviced by buses with capacity over 50?

SELECT DISTINCT teams.name FROM teams JOIN matches ON teams.team_id = matches.home_team_id JOIN goals ON matches.match_id = goals.match_id JOIN players ON goals.player_id = players.player_id WHERE strftime('%Y', matches.match_date) = '2023' GROUP BY matches.match_id HAVING SUM(CASE WHEN players.team_id = matches.home_team_id THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) > SUM(CASE WHEN players.team_id = matches.away_team_id THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) ORDER BY teams.name;
You are a text-to-SQL expert.

Question: Which stations have more temperature sensors than humidity sensors?

Question: Which pairs of users follow each other mutually? List the alphabetically first username and the second in each pair.

SELECT u1.username AS user1, u2.username AS user2 FROM follows f1 JOIN follows f2 ON f1.follower_id = f2.followee_id AND f1.followee_id = f2.follower_id JOIN users u1 ON f1.follower_id = u1.user_id JOIN users u2 ON f1.followee_id = u2.user_id WHERE u1.username < u2.username ORDER BY u1.username;

Question: For each doctor, what is the total cost (quantity times unit_price) of prescriptions issued in 2023 for medications with strength greater than 5, but only for doctors with more than 10 such prescriptions?

SELECT d.first_name || ' ' || d.last_name AS doctor_name, SUM(p.quantity * m.unit_price) AS total_cost FROM doctors d JOIN prescriptions p ON d.doctor_id = p.doctor_id JOIN medications m ON m.medication_id = p.medication_id WHERE strftime('%Y', p.prescribe_date) = '2023' AND m.strength > 5 GROUP BY d.doctor_id HAVING COUNT(p.prescription_id) > 10 ORDER BY total_cost DESC;

Question: Which dentists have more than 10 completed appointments involving procedures longer than 60 minutes in total across all their clinics in 2023?

SELECT d.first_name || ' ' || d.last_name AS dentist_name FROM dentists d JOIN appointments a ON d.dentist_id = a.dentist_id JOIN procedures pr ON a.procedure_id = pr.procedure_id WHERE a.status = 'completed' AND strftime('%Y', a.appt_date) = '2023' AND pr.duration > 60 GROUP BY d.dentist_id HAVING COUNT(a.appointment_id) > 10;

Question: For journals with impact factor greater than 3.0, list the journal names and the average number of citations received per article (only counting citations from 2023) for articles published after 2020, but only include journals where this average exceeds 5.

SELECT j.name, AVG(c.cnt) FROM journals j JOIN articles a ON j.journal_id = a.journal_id JOIN (SELECT cited_article_id, COUNT(*) as cnt FROM citations WHERE citation_year = 2023 GROUP BY cited_article_id) c ON a.article_id = c.cited_article_id WHERE j.impact_factor > 3.0 AND a.pub_year > 2020 GROUP BY j.journal_id, j.name HAVING AVG(c.cnt) > 5;
You are a text-to-SQL expert.

Question: For each theater, calculate the total revenue from bookings of highly rated movies (rating > 8), ordered by total revenue descending. Revenue is seats booked times ticket price.

SELECT t.name, SUM(b.seats_booked * s.ticket_price) AS total_revenue FROM theaters t JOIN showtimes s ON t.theater_id = s.theater_id JOIN bookings b ON s.showtime_id = b.showtime_id JOIN movies m ON s.movie_id = m.movie_id WHERE m.rating > 8 GROUP BY t.theater_id, t.name ORDER BY total_revenue DESC;

Question: For routes from SFO to LAX operated by airlines with a rating higher than 4.0, list each aircraft model and the number of flight assignments on those routes.

SELECT ac.model, COUNT(*) AS assignment_count FROM aircraft ac JOIN flight_assignments fa ON ac.aircraft_id = fa.aircraft_id JOIN flights f ON fa.flight_id = f.flight_id JOIN routes r ON f.route_id = r.route_id JOIN airports dep ON r.departure_airport_id = dep.airport_id JOIN airports arr ON r.arrival_airport_id = arr.airport_id JOIN airlines al ON f.airline_id = al.airline_id WHERE dep.iata_code = 'SFO' AND arr.iata_code = 'LAX' AND al.rating > 4.0 GROUP BY ac.model ORDER BY assignment_count DESC;
You are a text-to-SQL expert.

Question: For departure airports with more than 20 morning flights (before 12:00) in 2023 operated by US airlines, list the IATA code and the count of such flights.

SELECT a.iata_code, COUNT(f.flight_id) FROM airports a JOIN routes r ON a.airport_id = r.departure_airport_id JOIN flights f ON r.route_id = f.route_id JOIN airlines ai ON f.airline_id = ai.airline_id WHERE ai.country = 'USA' AND strftime('%Y', f.scheduled_date) = '2023' AND strftime('%H', f.scheduled_dep_time) < '12' GROUP BY a.airport_id, a.iata_code HAVING COUNT(f.flight_id) > 20 ORDER BY COUNT(f.flight_id) DESC;
You are a text-to-SQL expert.

WITH max_per_leaderboard AS (SELECT leaderboard_id, MAX(score_value) AS max_score FROM scores GROUP BY leaderboard_id) SELECT DISTINCT g.game_name FROM games g JOIN leaderboards l_pc ON g.game_id = l_pc.game_id JOIN leaderboards l_xbox ON g.game_id = l_xbox.game_id JOIN max_per_leaderboard mpl_pc ON l_pc.leaderboard_id = mpl_pc.leaderboard_id JOIN max_per_leaderboard mpl_xbox ON l_xbox.leaderboard_id = mpl_xbox.leaderboard_id WHERE g.release_date > '2020-01-01' AND l_pc.platform = 'PC' AND l_xbox.platform = 'Xbox' AND mpl_pc.max_score > mpl_xbox.max_score;

Question: Which customers have transactions categorized as 'grocery' but none categorized as 'salary'?

SELECT DISTINCT c.first_name || ' ' || c.last_name AS full_name FROM customers c WHERE EXISTS (SELECT 1 FROM accounts a JOIN transactions t ON a.account_id = t.account_id JOIN transaction_categories tc ON t.transaction_id = tc.transaction_id JOIN categories cat ON tc.category_id = cat.category_id WHERE a.customer_id = c.customer_id AND cat.category_name = 'grocery') AND NOT EXISTS (SELECT 1 FROM accounts a JOIN transactions t ON a.account_id = t.account_id JOIN transaction_categories tc ON t.transaction_id = tc.transaction_id JOIN categories cat ON tc.category_id = cat.category_id WHERE a.customer_id = c.customer_id AND cat.category_name = 'salary');
You are a text-to-SQL expert.

Question: Which users have more achievements than the average per user in RPG games and also have at least one score over 50000?

WITH rpg_avg AS (SELECT AVG(ach_count) AS avg_count FROM (SELECT COUNT(*) AS ach_count FROM user_achievements ua JOIN games g ON ua.game_id = g.game_id WHERE g.genre = 'RPG' GROUP BY ua.user_id)), rpg_above_avg AS (SELECT ua.user_id FROM user_achievements ua JOIN games g ON ua.game_id = g.game_id JOIN rpg_avg ON 1=1 WHERE g.genre = 'RPG' GROUP BY ua.user_id HAVING COUNT(*) > rpg_avg.avg_count) SELECT DISTINCT u.username FROM users u JOIN rpg_above_avg ra ON u.user_id = ra.user_id JOIN scores s ON u.user_id = s.user_id WHERE s.score > 50000;
You are a text-to-SQL expert.

Question: Which games have a higher average score on their weekly multiplayer leaderboards than on their monthly multiplayer leaderboards?

SELECT g.name FROM games g JOIN leaderboards lw ON g.game_id = lw.game_id JOIN scores sw ON lw.leaderboard_id = sw.leaderboard_id JOIN leaderboards lm ON g.game_id = lm.game_id JOIN scores sm ON lm.leaderboard_id = sm.leaderboard_id WHERE lw.mode = 'multiplayer' AND lw.period = 'weekly' AND lm.mode = 'multiplayer' AND lm.period = 'monthly' GROUP BY g.game_id, g.name HAVING AVG(sw.score) > AVG(sm.score);
You are a text-to-SQL expert.

Question: Which journals have more than 5 articles published in 2023 or later that each received at least 10 citations?

WITH article_cites AS (SELECT cited_article_id, COUNT(citation_id) AS num_cites FROM citations GROUP BY cited_article_id) SELECT j.name FROM journals j JOIN articles a ON j.journal_id = a.journal_id JOIN article_cites ac ON a.article_id = ac.cited_article_id WHERE strftime('%Y', a.pub_date) >= '2023' AND ac.num_cites >= 10 GROUP BY j.journal_id, j.name HAVING COUNT(a.article_id) > 5;
You are a text-to-SQL expert.

Question: Which cities have more than 20 residents between 25 and 45 years old employed in professional occupations?

SELECT cities.name FROM cities JOIN neighborhoods ON cities.city_id = neighborhoods.city_id JOIN households ON neighborhoods.neighborhood_id = households.neighborhood_id JOIN residents ON households.household_id = residents.household_id JOIN jobs ON residents.resident_id = jobs.resident_id WHERE age BETWEEN 25 AND 45 AND occupation = 'professional' GROUP BY cities.city_id, cities.name HAVING COUNT(resident_id) > 20;

Question: What is the total approved amount for each policyholder for approved claims from cardiology providers?

SELECT p.first_name || ' ' || p.last_name AS policyholder_name, SUM(c.approved_amount) FROM policyholders p JOIN policies po ON p.ph_id = po.ph_id JOIN claims c ON po.policy_id = c.policy_id JOIN providers pr ON c.provider_id = pr.provider_id WHERE c.status = 'approved' AND pr.specialty = 'cardiology' GROUP BY p.ph_id;

Question: List customers who have more than one claim, along with their total premiums and total claim amounts.

SELECT c.name, SUM(p.premium) AS total_premium, SUM(cl.amount) AS total_claims FROM customers c JOIN policies p ON c.customer_id = p.customer_id JOIN claims cl ON p.policy_id = cl.policy_id GROUP BY c.customer_id, c.name HAVING COUNT(cl.claim_id) > 1;

Question: Which patients had appointments with dentists specializing in orthodontics?

SELECT DISTINCT p.first_name || ' ' || p.last_name AS patient_name
FROM patients p
JOIN appointments a ON p.patient_id = a.patient_id
JOIN dentists d ON a.dentist_id = d.dentist_id
WHERE d.specialty = 'orthodontics';
You are a text-to-SQL expert.

Question: For each dental specialty, what is the total revenue from completed appointments in 2023 that lasted more than 30 minutes, showing only specialties with more than 5 such appointments? (Revenue is sum of treatment cost times quantity)

SELECT d.specialty, SUM(t.cost * at.quantity) AS total_revenue
FROM dentists d
JOIN appointments a ON d.dentist_id = a.dentist_id
JOIN appointment_treatments at ON a.appointment_id = at.appointment_id
JOIN treatments t ON at.treatment_id = t.treatment_id
WHERE a.status = 'completed'
  AND strftime('%Y', a.appointment_date) = '2023'
  AND a.duration_minutes > 30
GROUP BY d.specialty
HAVING COUNT(*) > 5;
You are a text-to-SQL expert.

Question: List the full names of students over age 21 who earned an 'A' in a Mathematics course during Fall 2023?

SELECT s.first_name || ' ' || s.last_name FROM students s JOIN enrollments e ON s.student_id = e.student_id JOIN courses c ON e.course_id = c.course_id JOIN departments d ON c.dept_id = d.dept_id WHERE s.age > 21 AND e.grade = 'A' AND d.name = 'Mathematics' AND c.semester = 'Fall 2023';
You are a text-to-SQL expert.

Question: Which team achieved the most podium finishes (positions 1, 2, or 3) in seasons 2020 or later?

Question: In the 2016 Summer Olympics, how many gold medals were awarded in each sport?

SELECT s.name, COUNT(m.medal_id) FROM medals m JOIN events e ON m.event_id = e.event_id JOIN olympic_games og ON e.games_id = og.games_id JOIN sports s ON e.sport_id = s.sport_id WHERE og.year = 2016 AND og.season = 'Summer' AND m.medal_type = 'Gold' GROUP BY s.sport_id, s.name ORDER BY COUNT(m.medal_id) DESC;

Question: For artists of each nationality, calculate the total sales revenue from artworks sold in 2023 during exhibitions that started after January 1, 2023. Only include nationalities with total revenue exceeding 50000.

SELECT ar.nationality, SUM(s.sale_price) FROM sales s JOIN artworks aw ON s.artwork_id = aw.artwork_id JOIN artists ar ON aw.artist_id = ar.artist_id JOIN exhibitions ex ON s.exhibition_id = ex.exhibition_id WHERE strftime('%Y', s.sale_date) = '2023' AND ex.start_date > '2023-01-01' GROUP BY ar.nationality HAVING SUM(s.sale_price) > 50000;
You are a text-to-SQL expert.

Question: Who are the male athletes from China that won medals in swimming events during the 2020 Olympics?

SELECT DISTINCT a.name FROM athletes a JOIN results r ON a.id = r.athlete_id JOIN events e ON r.event_id = e.id JOIN sports s ON e.sport_id = s.id JOIN olympics o ON e.olympics_id = o.id WHERE a.gender = 'M' AND a.country_id = (SELECT id FROM countries WHERE name = 'China') AND r.medal IN ('gold', 'silver', 'bronze') AND s.name = 'swimming' AND o.year = 2020;
You are a text-to-SQL expert.

Question: For the 2016 Summer Olympics, which countries won the most gold medals in athletics events, and what was their gold medal count? Show only those with more than 5 golds.

SELECT c.name, COUNT(*) as gold_count FROM results r JOIN athletes a ON r.athlete_id = a.id JOIN countries c ON a.country_id = c.id JOIN events e ON r.event_id = e.id JOIN sports s ON e.sport_id = s.id JOIN olympics o ON e.olympics_id = o.id WHERE r.medal = 'gold' AND s.name = 'athletics' AND o.year = 2016 AND o.season = 'summer' GROUP BY c.id, c.name HAVING gold_count > 5 ORDER BY gold_count DESC;
You are a text-to-SQL expert.

Question: Which patients had more than 2 appointments with orthodontists in 2023?

Question: For each branch that hosted more than 5 events in 2023, list the branch name, the number of such events, and the total registrations for those events, ordered by number of events descending.

SELECT l.branch_name, COUNT(e.event_id), COUNT(r.registration_id) FROM locations l JOIN events e ON l.location_id = e.location_id LEFT JOIN registrations r ON e.event_id = r.event_id WHERE strftime('%Y', e.event_date) = '2023' GROUP BY l.location_id, l.branch_name HAVING COUNT(e.event_id) > 5 ORDER BY COUNT(e.event_id) DESC;

Question: For each train type, what is the total revenue from confirmed tickets on schedules departing in 2023, but only for types with average ticket price over 100?

SELECT tr.type, SUM(t.price) AS total_revenue, AVG(t.price) AS avg_price FROM tickets t JOIN schedules s ON t.schedule_id = s.schedule_id JOIN trains tr ON s.train_id = tr.train_id WHERE t.status = 'confirmed' AND strftime('%Y', s.dep_date) = '2023' GROUP BY tr.type HAVING AVG(t.price) > 100;
You are a text-to-SQL expert.

SELECT b.title, a.name, p.name AS patron_name, l.fine_amount FROM loans l JOIN books b ON l.book_id = b.book_id JOIN genres g ON b.genre_id = g.genre_id JOIN authors a ON b.author_id = a.author_id JOIN patrons p ON l.patron_id = p.patron_id WHERE g.name = 'Mystery' AND (l.return_date IS NULL OR l.return_date > l.due_date);
You are a text-to-SQL expert.

Question: Which patients have visited more unique doctors than the average number of unique doctors per patient?

Question: Which users have more deposit transactions than the average number of deposit transactions per user?

SELECT u.first_name || ' ' || u.last_name FROM users u WHERE (SELECT COUNT(*) FROM transactions t JOIN accounts a ON t.account_id = a.account_id WHERE a.user_id = u.user_id AND t.trans_type = 'deposit') > (SELECT AVG(dep_cnt) FROM (SELECT COUNT(*) AS dep_cnt FROM transactions t JOIN accounts a ON t.account_id = a.account_id WHERE t.trans_type = 'deposit' GROUP BY a.user_id));
You are a text-to-SQL expert.

Question: For each genre, find the game with the highest total score sum across all its leaderboards.

SELECT g.genre, g.game_name, SUM(ps.score) as total_score
FROM games g
JOIN leaderboards l ON g.game_id = l.game_id
JOIN player_scores ps ON l.leaderboard_id = ps.leaderboard_id
GROUP BY g.game_id
HAVING total_score = (
  SELECT SUM(ps2.score)
  FROM games g2
  JOIN leaderboards l2 ON g2.game_id = l2.game_id
  JOIN player_scores ps2 ON l2.leaderboard_id = ps2.leaderboard_id
  WHERE g2.genre = g.genre
  ORDER BY SUM(ps2.score) DESC
  LIMIT 1
);
You are a text-to-SQL expert.

Question: Rank the top 3 players by their total score across all leaderboards for games released after '2022-01-01', including their country and total score.

SELECT p.username, p.country, SUM(ps.score) as total_score,
       ROW_NUMBER() OVER (ORDER BY SUM(ps.score) DESC) as rank
FROM players p
JOIN player_scores ps ON p.player_id = ps.player_id
JOIN leaderboards l ON ps.leaderboard_id = l.leaderboard_id
JOIN games g ON l.game_id = g.game_id
WHERE g.release_date > '2022-01-01'
GROUP BY p.player_id
HAVING rank <= 3;
You are a text-to-SQL expert.

Question: Which products have current inventory quantity less than the total quantity shipped to their warehouse in the last year, listing product name, warehouse name, current quantity, and total recent shipped?

SELECT p.name AS product_name, w.name AS warehouse_name, i.quantity AS current_qty, SUM(sh.quantity_shipped) AS recent_shipped FROM products p JOIN inventory i ON p.product_id = i.product_id JOIN warehouses w ON i.warehouse_id = w.warehouse_id JOIN shipments sh ON p.product_id = sh.product_id AND i.warehouse_id = sh.warehouse_id WHERE sh.shipment_date >= date('now', '-1 year') GROUP BY p.product_id, i.warehouse_id, i.quantity HAVING i.quantity < SUM(sh.quantity_shipped);
You are a text-to-SQL expert.

Question: For each state, find the party with the highest number of active voters born before 1980, and show the state, party name, and that count (only include states where this count is at least 50).

SELECT state_name.state, max_party.name, max_count FROM (SELECT d.state AS state, v.party_id, COUNT(*) AS cnt FROM voters v JOIN districts d ON v.district_id = d.district_id WHERE v.status = 'active' AND v.birth_date < '1980-01-01' GROUP BY d.state, v.party_id) AS subq JOIN (SELECT d.state AS state_name, MAX(cnt) AS max_cnt FROM (SELECT d.state, v.party_id, COUNT(*) AS cnt FROM voters v JOIN districts d ON v.district_id = d.district_id WHERE v.status = 'active' AND v.birth_date < '1980-01-01' GROUP BY d.state, v.party_id) GROUP BY state_name) AS maxes ON subq.state = maxes.state_name AND subq.cnt = maxes.max_cnt JOIN parties max_party ON subq.party_id = max_party.party_id WHERE max_count >= 50;
You are a text-to-SQL expert.

Question: Which team has the most wins? A win is when they score more goals than the opponent as home or away team.

SELECT t.team_name FROM teams t LEFT JOIN matches m ON (t.team_id = m.home_team_id OR t.team_id = m.away_team_id) GROUP BY t.team_id, t.team_name ORDER BY SUM(CASE WHEN (t.team_id = m.home_team_id AND m.home_goals > m.away_goals) OR (t.team_id = m.away_team_id AND m.away_goals > m.home_goals) THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) DESC LIMIT 1;
You are a text-to-SQL expert.

Question: Which carriers have shipped products from more than 3 different categories?

Question: List the full names of patients born after 1990 who were prescribed medications with strength greater than 10.

Question: For each department, what is the average net pay from 2023 paychecks where the bonus was greater than 0 and the employee was hired before 2021? Only include departments where this average exceeds 5000.

SELECT d.dept_name, AVG(p.net_pay) FROM payroll p JOIN employees e ON p.employee_id = e.employee_id JOIN departments d ON e.department_id = d.department_id WHERE strftime('%Y', p.pay_date) = '2023' AND p.bonus > 0 AND strftime('%Y', e.hire_date) < '2021' GROUP BY d.department_id HAVING AVG(p.net_pay) > 5000;
You are a text-to-SQL expert.

Question: For posts made by users over 25 years old with more than 50 likes, list the post content and the number of comments on each such post.

SELECT p.content, COUNT(c.comment_id) as comment_count FROM posts p JOIN users u ON p.user_id = u.user_id JOIN comments c ON p.post_id = c.post_id WHERE u.age > 25 AND p.likes_count > 50 GROUP BY p.post_id ORDER BY comment_count DESC;

Question: For each city, count the number of 2024 weddings that hired at least one vendor with rating higher than 4.5 and total cost over 20000, and give the average estimated guests for those weddings.

SELECT v.city, COUNT(DISTINCT w.wedding_id), AVG(w.estimated_guests) FROM venues v JOIN weddings w ON v.venue_id = w.venue_id JOIN wedding_vendors wv ON w.wedding_id = wv.wedding_id JOIN vendors vd ON wv.vendor_id = vd.vendor_id WHERE vd.rating > 4.5 AND strftime('%Y', w.wedding_date) = '2024' AND w.total_cost > 20000 GROUP BY v.city;

Question: Which team has the highest average points per race, considering only teams with at least 5 races?

SELECT t.name, AVG(rr.points) AS avg_points FROM teams t JOIN drivers d ON d.team_id = t.team_id JOIN race_results rr ON rr.driver_id = d.driver_id JOIN races r ON rr.race_id = r.race_id GROUP BY t.team_id, t.name HAVING COUNT(DISTINCT r.race_id) >= 5 ORDER BY avg_points DESC LIMIT 1;
You are a text-to-SQL expert.

Question: Which genres have an average track plays greater than 10000 for albums released after 2021, ordered by average plays descending?

SELECT a.genre, AVG(t.plays) AS avg_plays FROM artists a JOIN albums al ON a.artist_id = al.artist_id JOIN tracks t ON al.album_id = t.album_id WHERE strftime('%Y', al.release_date) > '2021' GROUP BY a.genre HAVING AVG(t.plays) > 10000 ORDER BY avg_plays DESC;

Question: Which county has the most approved registrations?

SELECT c.county_name FROM counties c JOIN voters v ON c.county_id = v.county_id JOIN registrations r ON v.voter_id = r.voter_id WHERE r.status = 'approved' GROUP BY c.county_id, c.county_name HAVING COUNT(r.reg_id) = (SELECT MAX(sub.cnt) FROM (SELECT COUNT(r2.reg_id) AS cnt FROM counties c2 JOIN voters v2 ON c2.county_id = v2.county_id JOIN registrations r2 ON v2.voter_id = r2.voter_id WHERE r2.status = 'approved' GROUP BY c2.county_id) AS sub);
You are a text-to-SQL expert.

Question: For the 2023 season, list the drivers and their total points, ordered by points descending.

SELECT d.first_name || ' ' || d.last_name AS driver_name, SUM(r.points) AS total_points
FROM drivers d JOIN results r ON d.driver_id = r.driver_id
JOIN races ra ON r.race_id = ra.race_id
WHERE ra.season = 2023
GROUP BY d.driver_id, driver_name
ORDER BY total_points DESC;

Question: Which active drivers have never achieved a podium finish (top 3 position) at a European circuit?

SELECT d.first_name || ' ' || d.last_name AS driver_name
FROM drivers d
WHERE d.is_active = 1
AND NOT EXISTS (
  SELECT 1 FROM results r JOIN races ra ON r.race_id = ra.race_id
  WHERE r.driver_id = d.driver_id AND r.position <= 3
  AND ra.location LIKE '%Europe%'
);
